title: Power Virtual Server
introduction: With the Power Virtual Server, you can quickly create and deploy one or more virtual servers (that are running either the AIX, IBM i, or Linux operating systems).

links.title: Getting started with IBM® Power® Virtual Servers
About IBM Power Virtual Server in IBM data center architecture, key features, and hardware specifications
About IBM Power Virtual Server Private Cloud in Client location architecture, key features, and hardware specifications
Migrating to an IBM Power11 system

section_highlights.title: IBM Power11 Launches in IBM Power Virtual Server
section_highlights.description: With the general availability of IBM Power11 in IBM Power Virtual Server and across all server classes - clients can unlock new levels of performance, flexibility, and resilience for their mission-critical workloads.

section_highlights.title: Connecting Power Virtual Server to Microsoft Azure
section_highlights.description: This topic provides a detailed guide on how to connect IBM Power Virtual Server to Microsoft Azure with Megaport.

tiles.title: Accelerated SAPS (aSAPS) is available as a performance measurement attribute for SAP profiles. aSAPS is an advanced SAP performance unit that represents system capacity based on the SAP quote‑to‑cash (Q2C) benchmark.
You can deploy VSIs for SAP NetWeaver workloads without using an SAP‑certified profile.
SAP Application Server profiles are supported on the IBM Power S1122 system.
IBM provides advanced or premium IBM cloud support for IBM Power Virtual Server Private Cloud in Client location.
CHE02 and CHE03 are available as Power Virtual Server data centers in Chennai, India.
